mymods_error_not_a_mod: That folder is not a mod - no ebr-mod.json found in {folder}.
mymods_error_unreadable_detail: Could not read the mod in {folder}: {detail}.
setup_lead: This app has no accounts or servers of its own - your mods live in your own free GitHub account. This one-time setup connects the app to GitHub so it can back up and publish what you make.
setup_credentials_lead: The GitHub sign-in registered on this computer. Used for backing up and publishing your mods.
setup_switch_confirm: This makes your computer forget the saved GitHub sign-in for github.com - system-wide, not just in this app. Every tool that uses it will be affected: the next git push, pull, or fetch (in any terminal, editor, or Git client), as well as publishing from this app, will prompt you to sign in again.
setup_forks_lead: Your own copies of the mod projects on GitHub, where your mods are stored and published from.
setup_forks_unreachable: At least one saved fork could not be reached on GitHub. It may have been deleted or renamed - run setup again to recreate it.
setup_manual_intro: Create these forks in your browser, then run setup again:
setup_author_lead: Pre-filled when you create a new mod.
setup_clear_lead: Removes your saved fork locations and author details from this computer. Forgets your GitHub sign-in for this app, but does not affect your system-wide GitHub credentials for other tools.
setup_error_no_sign_in: Could not detect a GitHub sign-in. These tools push to GitHub over HTTPS, so an HTTPS sign-in is required. Set one up (Git Credential Manager will prompt you), then try again.
setup_error_switch_manual: Could not clear the saved GitHub credential automatically. Clear it yourself with `git credential reject`, then run setup again.
mod_type_expansion_desc: Add expanded content to a campaign
newmod_lead: Fill in the details and choose where your new mod will be saved on your computer. The app clones your mod as a new branch in your GitHub fork, merges in the relevant official content, and creates placeholders for you.
newmod_setup_required: Finish setup before creating a mod. The app needs your creator forks first.
newmod_id_claimed: A mod with this ID already exists in the registry (by {author}). Consider renaming before you publish.
emoji_load_error: Could not load the emoji picker.
newmod_field_sets: Official path sets your mod uses
midcampaign_safe_label: Is your mod always safe to add to a campaign in progress?
midcampaign_notes_placeholder: eg. It starts on Day 10, so it's only safe to add mid-campaign before that.
newmod_location_hint: The mod will be created in a new "{folder}" subfolder here.
newmod_created_warnings: The mod was created, but some steps did not finish. You can retry them later from the mod's page.
newmod_warning_campaign_skipped: Skipped campaign {campaign} - add it later from the mod's page.
newmod_error_setup_required: Finish setup before creating a mod - the app needs your creator forks first.
newmod_error_invalid_language: Enter a valid language code (for example "en" or "pt-BR").
newmod_error_invalid_icon: The icon must be a single emoji, or left empty.
newmod_error_no_folder: Choose a folder for the new mod first.
moddetails_not_found: This mod could not be loaded. It may have been removed from your list.
moddetails_save_blocked: Not saved. Fix the highlighted fields.
unsaved_body: You have unsaved changes. What would you like to do?
moddetails_type_warning: Changing the mod type reclassifies your mod and can change how it appears in the registry. Make sure the mod's content matches the new type.
